sections.paragraph:
δίψος, ους, τὸ (s. διψάω; X., al.; Epict.; PTebt 272, 17 [med. pap]; LXX; s. Thackeray 157; Philo; Jos., Ant. 3, 37; 38; 18, 192. S. also Nägeli 14) thirst (w. λιμός, as Dio Chrys. 7 [8], 16; 13 [7], 55; Pythag., Ep. 4, 4; Socrat., Ep. 12; Is 5:13) 2 Cor 11:27.—B. 333. DELG s.v. δίψα. M-M. TW.

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5
  2. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

med. med. = medicus (medical writer), medical

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Pythag Pythag = letters attributed to the phil. Pythagoras (VI–V b.c.); s. Ep.; date uncertain—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle
  2. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 253
